spec_match.rationale: Demo invokes navigator.credentials.create with the payment extension from the top frame, but the actual spec change only fires in a cross-origin iframe without activation — the user is unlikely to ever see the renamed error here.

interactive.rationale: Real click handler that calls the WebAuthn API, classifies the caught error name, and renders a live verdict.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index promises a live probe of what the browser raises right now; the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: No cross-origin iframe variant, no activation variant, no path that would actually produce the old SecurityError on a pre-v137 build.

references.rationale: The see-also section now links to the correct ChromeStatus feature 5160752715137024, preserving the slug self-heal recovery path.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from CSS variables; verdict block reuses the design system tokens without overriding button colours.

openQuestions.title: Add a cross-origin iframe variant
openQuestions.detail: The actual v137 change only triggers in a cross-origin iframe without a user activation. The top-frame call shown here will not reproduce the pre-v137 SecurityError on any current Chrome. Wire in a sandboxed iframe path (mirroring `spc-error-probe`) so the demo can actually exercise the bug-fixed code path.

openQuestions.title: Add a no-activation branch
openQuestions.detail: The button-click handler always carries a transient activation, so the rename rarely fires. Add a second button that schedules the call via setTimeout / messageChannel so the activation has expired, giving the user a way to see the activation-gated error.

summary: Solid interactive probe, but it still needs a cross-origin iframe plus no-activation lane before the user can reliably see the renamed error path.
